specific_mechanism: Speleothems can record annual lamination via four mechanisms: fluorescent laminae (organic-matter flux variations), petrographic laminae (calcite texture or fabric variations), calcite-aragonite couplets, and trace element laminae. The annual signal is often imperfect (missed bands, false bands) due to hydrological irregularity
llm_application: A training-schedule analog: alternate four distinct sub-curricula (organic-flux-style varied-input, petrographic-style structural-input, couplet-style alternating-batch, trace-element-style sparse-tag-input) cyclically per 'year' of training; the model records its training history as a multi-mode banded structure analogous to speleothem laminae, allowing post-hoc paleoclimatic-style attribution of capability development to specific training-year features.
novelty_claim: Existing curriculum schedules use single-axis varying schedules. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a four-mode-banded annual-lamination-style curriculum where capability development is post-hoc attributable via stratigraphic-style band analysis.
